claimText: AmazonFACE is a Brazilian project that uses Free-Air CO2 Enrichment to simulate future atmospheric conditions and assess tropical forest adaptation to rising CO2.
evidenceExcerpt: AmazonFACE is a Brazilian project simulating future atmospheres to assess how tropical forests adapt to rising CO2 and temperatures.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the purpose and host country of the AmazonFACE project.
distractorHooks: Attributing the project to NASA or the USA
Describing it as a carbon capture and storage (CCS) technology rather than an adaptation study

claimText: Data from NASA's Orbiting Carbon Observatory (OCO) reveals that the Amazon rainforest currently emits more CO2 than it absorbs, acting as a net carbon source.
evidenceExcerpt: OCO findings reveal that the Amazon rainforest currently emits more CO2 than it absorbs.
examUse: Statement-based question on the current carbon sink status of major global biomes.
distractorHooks: Stating the Amazon rainforest is currently a net carbon sink
Confusing OCO findings with AmazonFACE objectives

claimText: Boreal forests and melting permafrost areas in Canada and Russia currently absorb more CO2 than they emit, acting as net carbon sinks.
evidenceExcerpt: Boreal forests and melting permafrost areas in Canada and Russia currently absorb more CO2 than they emit.
examUse: Comparison question contrasting the carbon sink status of the Amazon rainforest with boreal forests.
distractorHooks: Claiming boreal forests are net carbon emitters
Stating that melting permafrost areas in these regions currently emit more CO2 than they absorb
